questions.explanationJa: ASC 606-10-55-48 は、企業がブレイケージ額への権利を得ると見込まれる範囲でのみブレイケージの収益認識を認める。エスチート法（未請求財産法）により未使用残高を州へ納付する義務がある場合、企業はその金額への権利を有しないため、エスチート対象部分についてブレイケージ収益は認識できず、納付まで負債として計上し続ける。比例的認識は企業が保持する権利を持つブレイケージにのみ適用される。休眠期間満了時や販売時点での収益認識は、法的に州に帰属する金額を収益計上することになり不適切。

questions.subtopic: Debt Derecognition - Third-Party Assumption Without Legal Release
questions.stem: Brennan Corp. sells a warehouse to Tate Corp., and as part of the transaction Tate contractually agrees to assume and service Brennan's mortgage on the property. The lender, however, does not release Brennan from the note, and Brennan remains the primary obligor if Tate fails to pay. How should Brennan account for the mortgage after the sale?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Continue to report the mortgage as its liability because it has not been legally released by the lender

choices.label: B
choices.text: Derecognize the mortgage because Tate has contractually assumed the payments

choices.label: C
choices.text: Derecognize the mortgage and recognize a guarantee liability for the full mortgage balance

choices.label: D
choices.text: Report the mortgage net of the value of Tate's assumption agreement

questions.explanation: Under ASC 405-20-40-1, a debtor derecognizes a liability only when it pays the creditor or is legally released as primary obligor by the creditor or judicially. A private assumption agreement between buyer and seller does not bind the lender; Brennan remains primarily liable and must keep the mortgage on its balance sheet (with a corresponding receivable or other asset reflecting Tate's assumption, as applicable). Derecognition based on the assumption alone ignores the legal release requirement. Replacing the debt with a guarantee liability would be the accounting only if the lender had released Brennan and Brennan separately guaranteed Tate's performance. Netting the obligation against the assumption arrangement violates offsetting requirements.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 405-20-40-1 により、債務者が負債の認識を中止できるのは、債権者へ弁済した場合、または債権者もしくは裁判により主たる債務者の地位から法的に解放された場合に限られる。買主・売主間の引受契約は貸手を拘束しないため、Brennan は引き続き主たる債務者であり、モーゲージを貸借対照表に計上し続けなければならない（Tate の引受を反映する債権等を別途計上）。引受契約のみを根拠とする認識中止は法的免責要件を無視している。負債を保証債務に置き換える処理は、貸手が Brennan を免責し、Brennan が別途 Tate の履行を保証した場合にのみ妥当する。債務と引受契約の純額表示は相殺要件に違反する。

questions.subtopic: Convertible Debt Issued at a Substantial Premium
questions.stem: Ridgeline Corp. issues $1,000,000 face value convertible bonds for proceeds of $1,100,000. The conversion feature does not require bifurcation, and the 10% premium is considered substantial and attributable to the conversion feature. Under ASC 470-20, how should Ridgeline record the issuance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Liability of $1,100,000, with the premium amortized as a reduction of interest expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Liability of $1,000,000 and paid-in capital of $100,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Liability of $1,100,000, with the premium recognized immediately as a gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: Liability equal to the fair value of nonconvertible debt, with the remainder to paid-in capital

questions.explanation: ASC 470-20-25-13 retains a presumption (even after ASU 2020-06) that when convertible debt is issued at a substantial premium, the premium represents paid-in capital. Accordingly, the bonds are recorded at the $1,000,000 face amount as a liability, and the $100,000 substantial premium is credited to additional paid-in capital. Recording the entire $1,100,000 as a liability with premium amortization is the treatment for an ordinary (non-substantial) premium not attributable to the conversion feature. Recognizing the premium as an immediate gain has no GAAP basis — proceeds received at issuance are never income. Allocating based on the fair value of equivalent nonconvertible debt describes the repealed cash conversion model.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 470-20-25-13 は（ASU 2020-06 後も存続する規定として）、転換社債が実質的なプレミアム（substantial premium）付きで発行された場合、そのプレミアムは払込資本を表すという推定を置いている。したがって社債は額面 $1,000,000 を負債として計上し、実質的プレミアム $100,000 は資本剰余金（APIC）に貸方計上する。$1,100,000 全額を負債としプレミアムを利息費用の減額として償却する処理は、転換権に起因しない通常の（実質的でない）プレミアムの場合の取扱い。プレミアムの即時利得計上は発行時の受取額を収益とするものでGAAP上の根拠がない。非転換社債の公正価値で配分する方法は廃止されたキャッシュ・コンバージョン・モデルの説明である。

questions.subtopic: Purchase Commitment - Accrual of Loss on Firm Commitment
questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Moreland Corp. has a noncancelable commitment to purchase raw materials in Year 2 for a fixed price of $2,500,000. At year-end, the market value of the committed materials has declined to $2,200,000, and the decline is not expected to reverse before delivery. What should Moreland report in its Year 1 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Disclosure of the commitment only, with no loss recognized until the materials are purchased

choices.label: B
choices.text: A deferred charge of $300,000 to be added to inventory cost in Year 2

choices.label: C
choices.text: A loss of $300,000 and a corresponding liability for the loss on the purchase commitment

choices.label: D
choices.text: A direct write-down of existing inventory of $300,000

questions.explanation: Under ASC 330-10-35-17 through 35-18, a material loss on a firm, noncancelable purchase commitment is measured in the same way as an inventory write-down and is recognized in the period of the price decline: Moreland accrues a $300,000 loss ($2,500,000 - $2,200,000) and a liability (estimated loss on purchase commitment). Disclosure alone is appropriate only when no loss exists or the commitment is hedged or cancelable. Deferring the loss as a future inventory cost would capitalize a known loss, and writing down existing inventory is impossible because the goods have not yet been acquired — the loss attaches to the commitment, not to on-hand inventory.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 330-10-35-17〜18 により、解約不能の確定購入契約に係る重要な損失は、棚卸資産の評価減と同様の方法で測定し、価格下落が生じた期に認識する。Moreland は損失 $300,000（$2,500,000 − $2,200,000）と「購入契約損失引当金」（負債）を計上する。開示のみで足りるのは、損失が生じていない場合や契約がヘッジされている・解約可能な場合である。損失を将来の棚卸資産原価として繰り延べる処理は判明済みの損失の資産化に当たる。手持在庫の評価減は、対象物品が未取得である以上不可能で、損失は在庫ではなく契約に帰属する。

questions.stem: During Year 1, Corliss Products sells 200,000 units of a product, each carrying a $5 mail-in rebate offer. Based on experience with similar promotions, Corliss estimates that 30% of purchasers will redeem the rebate. Through December 31, Year 1, Corliss has paid $100,000 of rebates. What rebate liability should Corliss report at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Total estimated rebates = 200,000 units x 30% x $5 = $300,000, recognized as a reduction of the transaction price when the related sales occur. The year-end liability equals total estimated rebates minus rebates already paid: $300,000 - $100,000 = $200,000. $300,000 is the total estimated obligation before deducting the $100,000 of payments. $1,000,000 assumes every unit sold will be redeemed (200,000 x $5), ignoring the 30% redemption estimate. $100,000 is the amount already paid, which reduces the liability rather than representing the remaining obligation.
questions.explanationJa: 見積リベート総額 = 200,000個 × 30% × $5 = $300,000。リベートは変動対価として売上計上時に取引価格から減額する（ASC 606-10-32-8）。期末の負債残高 = 見積総額 − 既払額 = $300,000 − $100,000 = $200,000。$300,000 は既払 $100,000 を控除する前の見積総額。$1,000,000 は販売全数の償還（200,000 × $5）を仮定し30%の償還率見積りを無視した誤り。$100,000 は既払額そのもので、負債を減額する要素であり残存債務の額ではない。

questions.subtopic: Bonds Issued at Discount - Total Interest Expense Over Life
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Thackeray Corp. issues $1,000,000 face value, 6% bonds (interest paid annually) for $958,000. The bonds mature in 5 years. What total interest expense will Thackeray recognize over the entire life of the bonds?
questions.explanation: Total interest expense over the life of a bond equals total cash interest paid plus the discount (or minus the premium). Cash interest = $1,000,000 x 6% x 5 years = $300,000. Discount = $1,000,000 - $958,000 = $42,000, which is amortized into expense over the term. Total expense = $300,000 + $42,000 = $342,000. This is true regardless of whether straight-line or effective interest amortization is used; the methods change only the timing. $300,000 counts only the cash coupons and ignores the discount. $258,000 incorrectly subtracts the discount, which is the premium treatment. $42,000 is the discount alone, not total interest.
questions.explanationJa: 社債の全期間の利息費用合計 = 現金利息総額 ＋ ディスカウント（プレミアムの場合は控除）。現金利息 = $1,000,000 × 6% × 5年 = $300,000。ディスカウント = $1,000,000 − $958,000 = $42,000 で、これは期間にわたり利息費用へ償却される。合計費用 = $300,000 + $42,000 = $342,000（ASC 835-30）。この合計額は定額法・実効金利法のいずれでも同じで、両者の違いは期間配分（タイミング）のみ。$300,000 はクーポンのみでディスカウントを無視。$258,000 はディスカウントを控除しておりプレミアム発行の場合の扱い。$42,000 はディスカウント単独の額にすぎない。

questions.subtopic: Current Liability Classification - Operating Cycle Longer Than One Year
questions.stem: Garrick Shipbuilders has an operating cycle of 18 months. At year-end, Garrick has accounts payable for shipbuilding materials that are due in 15 months, consistent with the normal terms of its production cycle. How should these payables be classified on Garrick's classified balance sheet?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Noncurrent, because they are due more than 12 months after the balance sheet date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Current, because obligations expected to be settled within the normal operating cycle are current when the cycle exceeds one year

choices.label: C
choices.text: Split between current (the 12-month portion) and noncurrent (the remainder)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Noncurrent, unless Garrick intends to pay them early

questions.explanation: Current liabilities are obligations whose liquidation is reasonably expected to require the use of current assets within one year or the operating cycle, whichever is longer. Because Garrick's operating cycle is 18 months, payables due in 15 months — incurred in the normal course of the cycle — are current. A strict 12-month test ignores the longer-of rule in ASC 210-10. Splitting the balance by time has no basis when the entire obligation falls within the operating cycle. Classification depends on the expected settlement within the cycle, not on management's intent to accelerate payment.
questions.explanationJa: 流動負債は、1年または正常営業循環（operating cycle）のいずれか長い期間内に流動資産の使用により決済が見込まれる債務である（ASC 210-10-45-5〜8）。Garrick の営業循環は18か月であるため、循環の通常の過程で生じた15か月後支払の買掛金は流動負債となる。厳格な12か月基準は「いずれか長い方」ルールを無視している。債務全体が営業循環内に収まる以上、期間で按分する根拠はない。分類は循環内での決済見込みによって決まり、経営者の早期支払の意図には依存しない。

questions.subtopic: Accrued Interest Payable - Semiannual Bonds at Year-End
questions.stem: Loxley Corp. has $3,000,000 face value, 8% bonds outstanding that were issued at par. Interest is paid semiannually on April 1 and October 1. What accrued interest payable should Loxley report at December 31?
questions.explanation: The last interest payment was made October 1, so interest has accrued for 3 months (October, November, December) at year-end: $3,000,000 x 8% x 3/12 = $60,000. Because the bonds were issued at par, there is no discount or premium amortization to adjust the accrual. $120,000 represents a full 6 months of interest — the amount of each semiannual payment — rather than the 3 months accrued since October 1. $240,000 is a full year of interest. $20,000 reflects only 1 month of accrual ($3,000,000 x 8% x 1/12).
questions.explanationJa: 直近の利払日は10月1日であるため、期末時点で10月・11月・12月の3か月分の利息が未払いとして発生している：$3,000,000 × 8% × 3/12 = $60,000（ASC 835-30、発生主義）。額面発行のためディスカウント・プレミアムの償却による調整はない。$120,000 は半年ごとの利払額（6か月分）であり、10月1日以降に発生した3か月分ではない。$240,000 は1年分の利息。$20,000 は1か月分（$3,000,000 × 8% × 1/12）しか見ていない誤り。

questions.subtopic: Bonds Issued Between Interest Dates - Amortization Period
questions.stem: Corbett Corp.'s 10-year bonds are dated January 1, Year 1, but are not sold until March 1, Year 1, at a price that includes a premium. The bonds mature on December 31, Year 10. Over what period should Corbett amortize the bond premium?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 120 months, beginning January 1, Year 1

choices.label: B
choices.text: 118 months, beginning March 1, Year 1

choices.label: C
choices.text: 119 months, beginning February 1, Year 1

choices.label: D
choices.text: 60 months, beginning March 1, Year 1

questions.explanation: When bonds are sold after their dated date, any premium or discount is amortized over the period the bonds are actually outstanding — from the issuance date to the maturity date. Here, that period runs from March 1, Year 1 to December 31, Year 10, which is 118 months. Amortizing over the full 120-month stated term would incorrectly assign premium amortization to the two months (January and February of Year 1) during which the bonds were not outstanding and no interest expense was incurred.
questions.explanationJa: 社債が表示日（dated date）より後に売却された場合、打歩（プレミアム）や割引は実際に社債が流通している期間、すなわち発行日から満期日までの期間で償却する。本問では Year 1 の3月1日から Year 10 の12月31日までの118か月が償却期間となる。表示上の120か月で償却すると、社債が未発行で利息費用が発生していない Year 1 の1月・2月にも償却を割り当てることになり誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Bond Discount - Semiannual Effective Interest Total Year 1 Expense
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Delwood Corp. issues $2,000,000 face value, 6% bonds for $1,850,000. Interest is paid semiannually on June 30 and December 31, and the effective interest rate is 8% (4% per semiannual period). Using the effective interest method, what is total interest expense for Year 1?
questions.explanation: First period: interest expense = $1,850,000 × 4% = $74,000; cash interest = $2,000,000 × 3% = $60,000; discount amortized = $14,000, so the new carrying amount is $1,864,000. Second period: interest expense = $1,864,000 × 4% = $74,560. Total Year 1 expense = $74,000 + $74,560 = $148,560. The $120,000 amount is only the cash coupon and ignores discount amortization. The $148,000 amount fails to apply the effective rate to the increased second-period carrying amount. The $160,000 amount incorrectly applies the 8% effective rate to face value rather than to carrying value.
questions.explanationJa: 第1期: 利息費用 = $1,850,000 × 4% = $74,000、現金利息 = $2,000,000 × 3% = $60,000、割引償却 = $14,000 となり、帳簿価額は $1,864,000 に増加する。第2期: 利息費用 = $1,864,000 × 4% = $74,560。Year 1 合計 = $74,000 + $74,560 = $148,560。$120,000 は現金クーポンのみで割引償却を無視している。$148,000 は第2期に増加後の帳簿価額へ実効利率を適用していない。$160,000 は実効利率8%を帳簿価額ではなく額面に適用した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Bond Issue Costs with Discount - Initial Net Carrying Amount
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Fairmont Corp. issues $4,000,000 face value bonds at 98 and incurs $60,000 of bond issuance costs. At what net amount should the bonds initially be reported on the balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Debt issuance costs are presented as a direct deduction from the carrying amount of the debt, similar to a discount. Issue proceeds = $4,000,000 × 98% = $3,920,000. Net carrying amount = $3,920,000 − $60,000 issuance costs = $3,860,000. The $3,920,000 amount ignores the issuance costs, $4,000,000 ignores both the discount and the issuance costs, and $3,940,000 deducts the issuance costs from face value while ignoring the discount.
questions.explanationJa: 負債発行費用は割引と同様に負債の帳簿価額からの直接控除として表示する。発行収入 = $4,000,000 × 98% = $3,920,000。当初純帳簿価額 = $3,920,000 − 発行費用 $60,000 = $3,860,000。$3,920,000 は発行費用を無視しており、$4,000,000 は割引と発行費用の両方を無視している。$3,940,000 は額面から発行費用のみを控除し割引を無視した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Bond Retirement - Accrued Interest Included in Payment
questions.stem: Stanhope Corp. reacquires its outstanding bonds in the open market between interest dates. The bonds have a face value of $1,000,000 and unamortized discount of $40,000 at the reacquisition date. Stanhope pays total cash of $1,010,000, which includes $30,000 of accrued interest. What gain or loss should Stanhope recognize on the extinguishment?
questions.explanation: Accrued interest is not part of the reacquisition price; it settles the interest payable and is excluded from the extinguishment computation. Reacquisition price = $1,010,000 − $30,000 accrued interest = $980,000. Carrying amount = $1,000,000 − $40,000 unamortized discount = $960,000. Loss = $980,000 − $960,000 = $20,000. A $50,000 loss incorrectly compares the full $1,010,000 payment with the carrying amount. A $10,000 loss compares the total payment with face value, ignoring both the accrued interest and the discount. A $30,000 gain misclassifies the accrued interest itself as the extinguishment result.
questions.explanationJa: 経過利息は買戻価格に含めず、未払利息の決済として消滅損益の計算から除外する。買戻価格 = $1,010,000 − 経過利息 $30,000 = $980,000。帳簿価額 = $1,000,000 − 未償却割引 $40,000 = $960,000。損失 = $980,000 − $960,000 = $20,000。$50,000 の損失は支払総額 $1,010,000 を帳簿価額と比較した誤り。$10,000 の損失は支払総額と額面を比較し、経過利息と割引の両方を無視している。$30,000 の利得は経過利息そのものを消滅損益と誤認したものである。

questions.stem: In Year 1, Marbury Corp. retires bonds early and recognizes a material gain on the extinguishment. How should this gain be reported in Marbury's Year 1 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As an extraordinary item, net of tax

choices.label: B
choices.text: In other comprehensive income

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a component of income from continuing operations

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a direct adjustment to beginning retained earnings

questions.explanation: Gains and losses on early extinguishment of debt are reported in income from continuing operations in the period of extinguishment. The extraordinary item classification was eliminated from U.S. GAAP (ASU 2015-01), so net-of-tax extraordinary presentation is no longer permitted. Other comprehensive income is reserved for specifically enumerated items (such as certain pension adjustments and foreign currency translation), which do not include extinguishment gains. A direct adjustment to retained earnings is used for prior-period error corrections and certain accounting changes, not for a current-period transaction gain.
questions.explanationJa: 負債の期限前消滅に係る損益は、消滅した期の継続事業からの利益（income from continuing operations）に計上する。異常項目（extraordinary item）の区分は ASU 2015-01 により米国基準から廃止されており、税引後の異常項目表示は認められない。その他の包括利益は年金調整や外貨換算調整など限定列挙された項目のためのものであり、消滅利得は含まれない。利益剰余金への直接調整は過年度の誤謬訂正等に用いるものであり、当期の取引利得には適用されない。

questions.subtopic: Debt Exchange - Extinguishment with New Debt at Fair Value
questions.stem: Renfrew Corp. is having difficulty servicing a note payable, although it is not experiencing financial difficulty for accounting purposes. Renfrew and its lender exchange the old note, which has a carrying amount of $5,200,000 (including $200,000 of unamortized premium), for a new note with substantially different terms — the present value of the cash flows under the new note differs from the present value of the remaining old cash flows by more than 10%. The fair value of the new note is $4,900,000. What should Renfrew recognize at the exchange date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No gain or loss; the new note is recorded at $5,200,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: A gain of $100,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: A gain of $300,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: A loss of $300,000

questions.explanation: Because the present value of the cash flows changes by more than 10%, the exchange is accounted for as an extinguishment: the old debt is derecognized and the new debt is recorded at its fair value of $4,900,000. Gain = carrying amount of old debt $5,200,000 − fair value of new debt $4,900,000 = $300,000 gain. Recording the new note at the old carrying amount with no gain or loss is the treatment for a modification (a change of less than 10%), which does not apply here. A $100,000 gain incorrectly uses the $5,000,000 face amount instead of the $5,200,000 carrying amount. A loss is the wrong direction because the new obligation is smaller than the carrying amount extinguished.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュ・フローの現在価値が10%超変動しているため、本取引は消滅（extinguishment）として処理する。旧債務の認識を中止し、新債務を公正価値 $4,900,000 で計上する。利得 = 旧債務帳簿価額 $5,200,000 − 新債務公正価値 $4,900,000 = $300,000。損益を認識せず新債務を旧帳簿価額で計上するのは変動が10%未満の条件変更（modification）の処理であり、本問には当てはまらない。$100,000 の利得は帳簿価額 $5,200,000 ではなく額面 $5,000,000 を用いた誤り。新債務が消滅した帳簿価額より小さいため、損失となる方向も誤りである。

questions.stem: Hadley Corp. issues bonds with stock warrants attached. The warrants are nondetachable — they cannot be separated from the bonds and traded independently, and the bonds must be surrendered to exercise the warrants. How should Hadley allocate the issuance proceeds?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Allocate the entire proceeds to the bonds, with no amount assigned to the warrants

choices.label: B
choices.text: Allocate the proceeds between the bonds and the warrants based on relative fair values

choices.label: C
choices.text: Allocate to the warrants their fair value, with the residual assigned to the bonds

choices.label: D
choices.text: Allocate the entire proceeds to paid-in capital

questions.explanation: Securities with nondetachable warrants are accounted for in the same manner as convertible debt: the instrument is treated as a single unit and the entire proceeds are recorded as debt, with no separate amount assigned to the equity feature. Allocation between debt and equity using relative fair values, or assigning the warrants their fair value with the residual to the bonds, applies only when the warrants are detachable and can trade separately. Recording the entire proceeds in paid-in capital ignores the debt obligation entirely.
questions.explanationJa: 分離不能ワラント付き証券は転換社債と同様に処理する。すなわち単一の金融商品として全発行収入を負債に計上し、持分部分に金額を配分しない。相対公正価値による負債・持分への配分や、ワラントに公正価値を配分し残額を社債とする方法は、ワラントが分離可能で独立して取引できる場合にのみ適用される。全額を払込資本に計上する処理は負債としての義務を完全に無視している。

questions.subtopic: Convertible Bond Conversion - Market Value Method
questions.stem: Lockhart Corp. has convertible bonds with a face value of $1,000,000 and a carrying amount of $980,000. Bondholders convert all of the bonds into 50,000 shares of Lockhart's $5 par common stock when the stock's market price is $21 per share. Lockhart uses the market value method to record the conversion. What gain or loss should Lockhart recognize?
questions.explanation: Under the market value method, the stock issued is recorded at its fair value, and the difference between that fair value and the carrying amount of the converted bonds is recognized in income. Fair value of stock = 50,000 shares × $21 = $1,050,000. Loss = $1,050,000 − $980,000 carrying amount = $70,000 loss (the company gave up equity worth more than the recorded liability). Recognizing no gain or loss is the result under the book value method, not the market value method. A $50,000 loss incorrectly compares the stock's fair value with the $1,000,000 face value rather than the carrying amount. A gain is the wrong direction because the fair value of the shares issued exceeds the carrying amount of the debt removed.
questions.explanationJa: 時価法（market value method）では、交付株式を公正価値で計上し、その公正価値と転換社債の帳簿価額との差額を損益に認識する。株式の公正価値 = 50,000株 × $21 = $1,050,000。損失 = $1,050,000 − 帳簿価額 $980,000 = $70,000（計上されていた負債を上回る価値の持分を引き渡したため損失）。損益を認識しないのは簿価法（book value method）の結果である。$50,000 の損失は帳簿価額ではなく額面 $1,000,000 と比較した誤り。交付株式の公正価値が消滅する負債の帳簿価額を上回っているため、利得とする方向も誤りである。

questions.stem: Several years ago, Pemrose Corp. issued bonds with detachable stock warrants and credited $150,000 of the proceeds to additional paid-in capital — stock warrants. The warrants have now expired unexercised. How should Pemrose account for the expiration?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a $150,000 gain in income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reclassify the $150,000 to a liability for potential claims

choices.label: C
choices.text: Transfer the $150,000 to another paid-in capital account, with no effect on income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Add the $150,000 back to the carrying amount of the bonds

questions.explanation: Amounts received for equity instruments remain in equity even if the instruments expire unexercised. The balance in paid-in capital from the warrants is simply transferred to another paid-in capital account (such as paid-in capital from expired warrants); total equity and net income are unaffected. Recognizing a gain in income is prohibited because transactions in an entity's own equity instruments do not produce income. No liability arises on expiration because the holders' rights have lapsed. Adding the amount to the bonds' carrying amount is wrong because the original allocation to the warrants is not reversed — the bonds continue to be accounted for based on their allocated issue price.
questions.explanationJa: 持分金融商品の対価として受け取った金額は、その商品が未行使のまま失効しても資本にとどまる。ワラントに係る払込資本の残高は別の払込資本勘定（失効ワラントからの払込資本等）へ振り替えるだけで、資本合計にも純利益にも影響しない。自社の持分商品に係る取引から利益は生じないため、損益への利得計上は認められない。保有者の権利は消滅しているため負債も発生しない。当初のワラントへの配分は取り消されないため、社債の帳簿価額へ戻し入れる処理も誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Note Payable with Below-Market Stated Rate
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Whitcomb Corp. purchases equipment by issuing a $300,000, 3-year note payable bearing interest at 3%, payable annually. The prevailing market rate for similar notes is 9%. The present value of $1 at 9% for 3 periods is 0.77, and the present value of an ordinary annuity of $1 at 9% for 3 periods is 2.53. At what amount should Whitcomb record the equipment?
questions.explanation: When a note's stated rate is unreasonably below the market rate, the note and the asset acquired are recorded at the present value of the note's cash flows discounted at the market rate. Annual stated interest = $300,000 × 3% = $9,000. Present value = ($9,000 × 2.53) + ($300,000 × 0.77) = $22,770 + $231,000 = $253,770. Recording the equipment at the $300,000 face amount ignores the imputation requirement because the 3% stated rate does not represent the market rate. The $231,000 amount discounts only the principal and omits the stated interest payments. The $327,000 amount incorrectly adds the total stated interest of $27,000 to the face amount instead of discounting the cash flows.
questions.explanationJa: 手形の表面利率が市場利率を不合理に下回る場合、手形と取得資産は市場利率で割り引いたキャッシュ・フローの現在価値で計上する。年間表面利息 = $300,000 × 3% = $9,000。現在価値 = ($9,000 × 2.53) + ($300,000 × 0.77) = $22,770 + $231,000 = $253,770。額面 $300,000 での計上は、表面利率3%が市場利率を表さないため利息の擬制（imputation）を無視している。$231,000 は元本のみを割り引き表面利息の支払を omit した誤り。$327,000 は割引計算をせず表面利息総額 $27,000 を額面に加算した誤りである。

questions.subtopic: Zero-Coupon Note - Total Interest Over Life
questions.stem: Granger Corp. issues a 5-year, zero-coupon note with a face value of $500,000 for proceeds of $340,000. What is the total interest expense Granger will recognize over the life of the note?
questions.explanation: A zero-coupon note pays no periodic interest, so the entire difference between the face value repaid at maturity and the proceeds received at issuance is interest: $500,000 − $340,000 = $160,000, recognized over the 5-year life using the effective interest method. Reporting zero total interest confuses the absence of cash coupons with the absence of interest expense. The $32,000 amount is only one year's straight-line share ($160,000 ÷ 5), not the total over the life. The $200,000 amount does not correspond to the actual discount on this note.
questions.explanationJa: ゼロクーポン債は定期利息を支払わないため、満期に返済する額面と発行時の手取額との差額の全額が利息となる: $500,000 − $340,000 = $160,000。これを5年間にわたり実効金利法で認識する。利息合計をゼロとするのは、現金クーポンがないことと利息費用がないことを混同している。$32,000 は定額法による1年分（$160,000 ÷ 5）にすぎず、全期間の合計ではない。$200,000 はこの手形の実際の割引額と一致しない。

questions.subtopic: Installment Note - Current Portion at Year-End
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Tilford Corp. borrows $1,000,000 on a 5-year mortgage note bearing interest at 6%, with equal annual payments of $237,400 due each December 31. After making the Year 1 payment, what amount should Tilford report as the current portion of the note at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: Year 1: interest = $1,000,000 × 6% = $60,000; principal reduction = $237,400 − $60,000 = $177,400; balance at December 31, Year 1 = $822,600. The current portion is the principal that will be repaid in Year 2: Year 2 interest = $822,600 × 6% = $49,356, so Year 2 principal = $237,400 − $49,356 = $188,044. The $177,400 amount is the principal already repaid in Year 1, not the amount due next year. The $237,400 amount is the full payment, which includes interest that has not yet accrued and is therefore not a year-end liability. The $822,600 amount is the entire remaining balance, most of which is noncurrent.
questions.explanationJa: Year 1: 利息 = $1,000,000 × 6% = $60,000、元本返済 = $237,400 − $60,000 = $177,400、Year 1 末残高 = $822,600。流動部分は Year 2 に返済される元本である: Year 2 利息 = $822,600 × 6% = $49,356、よって Year 2 元本 = $237,400 − $49,356 = $188,044。$177,400 は Year 1 に既に返済済みの元本であり、翌年返済額ではない。$237,400 は支払額全額で、まだ発生していない利息を含むため期末負債にならない。$822,600 は残高全体であり、その大部分は非流動である。

questions.stem: Under ASC 410-20, a company revises its estimate of the undiscounted cash flows required to settle an asset retirement obligation downward. What discount rate should the company use to measure the decrease in the liability?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The current credit-adjusted risk-free rate at the date of the revision

choices.label: B
choices.text: The credit-adjusted risk-free rate that existed when the obligation was originally recognized

choices.label: C
choices.text: The risk-free rate without any credit adjustment

choices.label: D
choices.text: The company's weighted-average cost of capital

questions.explanation: ASC 410-20 uses a layered approach: upward revisions in estimated cash flows are treated as a new layer of the liability and discounted at the current credit-adjusted risk-free rate, while downward revisions reduce existing layers and are discounted at the rate (or weighted-average rate) that applied when those layers were originally recognized. Using the current rate for a decrease applies the upward-revision rule to the wrong direction. A pure risk-free rate is never used because ASC 410-20 requires the rate to be adjusted for the entity's credit standing. The weighted-average cost of capital is not a permitted measurement rate for AROs.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 410-20 はレイヤー方式を採用しており、見積キャッシュ・フローの増額改訂は負債の新たなレイヤーとして改訂時点の信用調整後リスクフリーレートで割り引く一方、減額改訂は既存レイヤーの減少として、そのレイヤーを当初認識した時点のレート（または加重平均レート）で割り引く。減額に現在のレートを使うのは増額改訂のルールを誤った方向に適用したもの。ASC 410-20 は企業の信用状態を反映したレートを要求するため、信用調整のない純粋なリスクフリーレートは使用しない。加重平均資本コストはAROの測定に認められたレートではない。

questions.stem: Which of the following statements about asset retirement obligations (AROs) under ASC 410-20 is correct?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The ARO is measured at the undiscounted amount of the estimated future retirement costs

choices.label: B
choices.text: The retirement cost is expensed immediately when the obligation is incurred

choices.label: C
choices.text: The ARO is recognized only when the asset is actually retired

choices.label: D
choices.text: The ARO is recognized at fair value when incurred, with a corresponding increase to the carrying amount of the related asset

questions.explanation: An ARO is recognized in the period in which a legal obligation is incurred, provided its fair value can be reasonably estimated, and is measured at fair value — typically the present value of the expected retirement cash flows discounted at a credit-adjusted risk-free rate. The same amount is capitalized as an asset retirement cost, increasing the carrying amount of the related long-lived asset, and is then depreciated over the asset's useful life. Measuring at undiscounted cash flows ignores the fair value (present value) requirement. Immediate expensing contradicts the capitalization requirement. Waiting until actual retirement to recognize the liability defers an existing legal obligation and understates liabilities throughout the asset's life.
questions.explanationJa: AROは法的義務が発生した期に、公正価値を合理的に見積もれる場合に認識し、公正価値（通常は信用調整後リスクフリーレートで割り引いた予想除去キャッシュ・フローの現在価値）で測定する。同額を資産除去費用として資産化し、関連する長期性資産の帳簿価額を増額したうえで耐用年数にわたり減価償却する。割引前キャッシュ・フローでの測定は公正価値（現在価値）の要求を無視している。即時費用処理は資産化の要求に反する。実際の除去時まで認識を遅らせる処理は、既に存在する法的義務の認識を繰り延べ、資産の使用期間を通じて負債を過小表示する。

questions.stem: Norfield Corp. does not carry insurance against hurricane damage to its coastal facilities and instead self-insures. Based on historical patterns, management expects average annual storm losses of $400,000 and wishes to accrue this amount each year to smooth earnings. No storm damage has occurred as of year-end. What is the appropriate accounting?

choices.label: A
choices.text: No accrual is recorded, because no asset has been impaired and no liability has been incurred at the balance sheet date

choices.label: B
choices.text: Accrue $400,000 each year as a self-insurance reserve

choices.label: C
choices.text: Accrue the present value of expected losses over the facilities' remaining lives

choices.label: D
choices.text: Record the $400,000 in other comprehensive income until a storm occurs

questions.explanation: ASC 450 prohibits accrual for general or unspecified business risks, including self-insured risks for events that have not yet occurred. A loss contingency is accrued only when an asset has been impaired or a liability incurred as of the balance sheet date — exposure to future storms does not meet that condition because the damaging event has not happened. Accruing an annual reserve, whether at the expected amount or at present value, creates a reserve for future losses and improperly smooths income. Other comprehensive income is not a holding area for unincurred losses; the exposure may be disclosed, but no amount is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 450 は、一般的・不特定の事業リスク（未発生の事象に対する自家保険リスクを含む）に対する引当を禁止している。損失偶発事象は、貸借対照表日時点で資産が毀損しているか負債が発生している場合にのみ引当計上する。将来の暴風被害へのエクスポージャーは、損害事象がまだ発生していないためこの条件を満たさない。期待額であれ現在価値であれ毎期引当金を積む処理は、将来損失のための準備金を創設し利益を不当に平準化するものである。その他の包括利益は未発生損失の待避場所ではない。エクスポージャーは開示し得るが、金額は認識しない。

questions.subtopic: Subsequent Events - Condition Arising After Year-End
questions.stem: Calverton Corp.'s fiscal year ends December 31, Year 1. On January 20, Year 2, before the financial statements are issued, a flood destroys one of Calverton's uninsured warehouses, resulting in a material loss. The flood was an isolated event with no connection to conditions existing at December 31, Year 1. How should Calverton treat this event in its Year 1 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Accrue the loss in the Year 1 financial statements

choices.label: B
choices.text: Accrue the loss and also disclose the event in the notes

choices.label: C
choices.text: Neither accrue nor disclose, because the event occurred after year-end

choices.label: D
choices.text: Disclose the nature of the event and an estimate of the loss in the notes, without accrual

questions.explanation: This is a nonrecognized subsequent event: the condition (the flood) arose after the balance sheet date, so it provides no evidence about conditions existing at December 31, Year 1, and no accrual is made in Year 1. Because the event is material and the statements would otherwise be misleading, Calverton must disclose the nature of the event and an estimate of its financial effect (or state that an estimate cannot be made). Accruing the loss, with or without disclosure, is appropriate only for recognized subsequent events that confirm conditions existing at the balance sheet date. Omitting disclosure entirely is wrong because a material post-year-end event of this kind must be disclosed even though it is not recognized.
questions.explanationJa: これは非認識後発事象である。状況（洪水）が貸借対照表日後に発生しているため、Year 1 の12月31日時点に存在した状況の証拠とはならず、Year 1 では引当計上しない。事象は重要であり開示しなければ財務諸表が誤解を招くため、事象の性質と財務的影響の見積額（または見積不能である旨）を注記で開示しなければならない。損失の引当計上（開示の有無を問わず）は、貸借対照表日時点に存在した状況を裏付ける認識後発事象にのみ適切である。重要な期末後事象であるため、開示自体を省略するのも誤りである。

questions.stem: In December Year 1, a jury renders a verdict against Brindle Corp. for $2,000,000 in a product liability case. Brindle files an appeal. Legal counsel advises that it is probable the verdict will be upheld on appeal, although it is reasonably possible the appellate court could reduce the award to $1,200,000. What amount should Brindle accrue at December 31, Year 1?
questions.explanation: A loss is accrued when it is probable and reasonably estimable. Counsel believes it is probable the $2,000,000 verdict will be upheld, so $2,000,000 is the best estimate of the loss and should be accrued; the possible reduction to $1,200,000 is only reasonably possible and therefore does not change the measurement, though it may be disclosed. Accruing nothing ignores a probable, estimable loss evidenced by an actual jury verdict. The $800,000 amount is merely the difference between the verdict and the possible reduced award and has no measurement significance. Accruing $1,200,000 treats the reasonably possible reduction as the best estimate, but the minimum-of-the-range approach applies only when no amount within the range is more likely than the others — here the full verdict is the probable outcome.
questions.explanationJa: 損失は発生の可能性が高く（probable）合理的に見積可能な場合に引当計上する。弁護士は $2,000,000 の評決が控訴審で維持される可能性が高いと判断しているため、$2,000,000 が損失の最善の見積りであり、これを引当計上する。$1,200,000 への減額は合理的に起こり得る（reasonably possible）にすぎず測定額を変えないが、開示の対象にはなり得る。引当をしない処理は、現実の陪審評決という証拠のある probable かつ見積可能な損失を無視している。$800,000 は評決額と減額可能性のある金額との差額にすぎず、測定上の意味を持たない。$1,200,000 の引当は reasonably possible な減額を最善の見積りとして扱うものだが、レンジの最小値を用いる方法はレンジ内のどの金額も他より可能性が高いとはいえない場合にのみ適用される。本問では評決全額が probable な結果である。

questions.subtopic: Warranty Expense - Full Recognition in Year of Sale
questions.stem: Ashbury Corp. sells appliances with a 2-year assurance-type warranty. Based on experience, warranty costs are expected to equal 1% of sales in the year of sale and 3% of sales in the year following the sale. Year 1 sales were $3,000,000, and Ashbury paid $25,000 of actual warranty claims during Year 1. What warranty expense should Ashbury report for Year 1?
questions.explanation: Under the expense warranty (accrual) approach, the entire estimated cost of servicing warranties on products sold during the year is expensed in the year of sale to match the cost against the related revenue: ($3,000,000 × 1%) + ($3,000,000 × 3%) = $30,000 + $90,000 = $120,000. The $25,000 amount is the cash-basis claims paid, which is not the expense. The $30,000 amount includes only the costs expected in the year of sale and improperly defers the costs expected next year, even though they relate to Year 1 sales. The $90,000 amount captures only the second-year portion and omits the current-year portion.
questions.explanationJa: 費用認識アプローチ（引当法）では、当期に販売した製品の保証に要する見積総額を販売年度に費用計上し、関連する収益と対応させる: ($3,000,000 × 1%) + ($3,000,000 × 3%) = $30,000 + $90,000 = $120,000。$25,000 は現金主義で支払ったクレーム額であり費用ではない。$30,000 は販売年度に発生が見込まれるコストのみを含み、Year 1 の売上に関連するにもかかわらず翌年度見込分を不当に繰り延べている。$90,000 は2年目部分のみで当年度部分を欠いている。

questions.stem: Kelridge Corp. launches an entirely new product line in December Year 1 with a 3-year warranty. Because the product uses unproven technology and Kelridge has no claims history or comparable industry data, management concludes that warranty costs are probable but cannot be reasonably estimated. What should Kelridge do in its Year 1 financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Accrue an amount based on the warranty percentage used for its other product lines

choices.label: B
choices.text: Disclose the warranty obligation in the notes without accruing a liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: Accrue the maximum possible warranty exposure

choices.label: D
choices.text: Neither accrue nor disclose until claims are actually received

questions.explanation: Accrual of a loss contingency requires that the loss be both probable and reasonably estimable. When warranty costs are probable but no reasonable estimate can be made, no liability is accrued; instead, the nature of the contingency is disclosed, along with the fact that an estimate cannot be made. Borrowing the warranty percentage from dissimilar product lines does not produce a reasonable estimate for unproven technology. Accruing the maximum exposure is not a faithful measurement and overstates liabilities. Remaining silent until claims arrive fails the disclosure requirement that applies when a probable loss cannot be estimated.
questions.explanationJa: 損失偶発事象の引当計上には、損失が probable かつ合理的に見積可能であることの両方が必要である。保証費用の発生は probable だが合理的な見積りができない場合、負債は計上せず、偶発事象の性質と見積不能である旨を注記で開示する。性質の異なる他の製品ラインの保証率を流用しても、未実証技術に対する合理的な見積りにはならない。最大エクスポージャーの引当は忠実な測定ではなく負債の過大表示となる。クレームが実際に届くまで何もしないのは、probable な損失が見積不能な場合に求められる開示要件に反する。

questions.subtopic: Compensated Absences - Sick Pay vs. Vacation
questions.stem: Under ASC 710-10, an employer's obligation for which of the following compensated absences MUST be accrued (assuming the obligation relates to services already rendered, payment is probable, and the amount is reasonably estimable)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sick pay benefits that vest — employees are paid for unused sick days upon termination

choices.label: B
choices.text: Sick pay benefits that accumulate but do not vest

choices.label: C
choices.text: Vacation benefits that neither vest nor accumulate

choices.label: D
choices.text: Holiday pay for future company holidays

questions.explanation: Accrual is required when the obligation relates to past services, the rights vest or accumulate, payment is probable, and the amount is reasonably estimable. Sick pay that vests must be accrued because employees will be paid regardless of future illness. For sick pay that accumulates but does not vest, accrual is permitted but specifically not required — ASC 710-10 provides this exception because payment depends on future illness. Benefits that neither vest nor accumulate fail the vesting/accumulation criterion entirely, so no accrual is made. Pay for future holidays relates to future services, not services already rendered.
questions.explanationJa: 引当計上が要求されるのは、債務が過去の勤務に関連し、権利が確定（vest）または累積（accumulate）し、支払の可能性が高く、金額を合理的に見積もれる場合である。権利確定する病気休暇は、将来の疾病の有無にかかわらず支払われるため引当計上が必須である。累積するが権利確定しない病気休暇については、支払が将来の疾病に依存するため、ASC 710-10 は例外として引当を任意（許容されるが要求されない）としている。権利確定も累積もしない給付はそもそも要件を満たさず引当しない。将来の祝日に係る給与は既に提供された勤務ではなく将来の勤務に関連する。

questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Donnelly Corp. has a $5,000,000 note payable maturing in June Year 2. Before the financial statements are issued, Donnelly enters into a financing agreement with a bank that would allow it to refinance the note on a long-term basis. However, the agreement permits the bank to cancel its commitment if, in the bank's sole judgment, a material adverse change occurs in Donnelly's financial condition. How should Donnelly classify the note at December 31, Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Entirely as a current liability

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entirely as a noncurrent liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: As noncurrent, with the cancellation clause disclosed in the notes

choices.label: D
choices.text: Half current and half noncurrent

questions.explanation: To exclude a short-term obligation from current liabilities based on a refinancing agreement, the agreement must not be cancelable by the lender, and compliance with its conditions must be objectively determinable. A clause allowing the bank to withdraw based on its own subjective judgment of a material adverse change means the refinancing is not assured, so the note remains a current liability in full. Noncurrent classification — with or without disclosure of the clause — overstates the company's ability to refinance because disclosure cannot cure the failure of the noncancelability condition. An arbitrary split between current and noncurrent has no basis; partial noncurrent classification is appropriate only when a valid agreement covers part of the obligation.
questions.explanationJa: 借換契約に基づき短期債務を流動負債から除外するには、契約が貸し手によって解約不能であり、契約条件の充足が客観的に判定可能でなければならない。銀行が自らの主観的判断で重大な悪化（material adverse change）を理由にコミットメントを取り消せる条項がある場合、借換は確実とはいえず、手形は全額流動負債のままとする。条項の注記開示の有無にかかわらず非流動への分類は借換能力の過大表示であり、開示によって解約不能要件の欠如を治癒することはできない。流動・非流動への恣意的な折半にも根拠はない。一部のみの非流動分類は、有効な契約が債務の一部のみをカバーする場合に限り適切である。

questions.subtopic: Debt to Be Retired from a Noncurrent Sinking Fund
questions.stem: At December 31, Year 1, Wexler Corp. has bonds payable of $2,000,000 maturing on September 30, Year 2. The bonds will be retired entirely from a bond sinking fund that is classified as a noncurrent investment on Wexler's balance sheet. How should the bonds be classified at December 31, Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a current liability

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a noncurrent liability

choices.label: C
choices.text: As a current liability, net of the sinking fund balance

choices.label: D
choices.text: As a deduction from the sinking fund investment

questions.explanation: A liability is classified as current when its liquidation is expected to require the use of existing current assets or the creation of other current liabilities. Because these bonds will be retired from a fund classified as a noncurrent asset — not from current assets — they are properly reported as noncurrent even though they mature within one year. Classifying them as current contradicts the definition because no current assets will be used. Netting the bonds against the sinking fund, whether shown in current liabilities or as a deduction from the investment, violates the general prohibition against offsetting assets and liabilities absent a right of setoff.
questions.explanationJa: 負債は、その決済に既存の流動資産の使用または他の流動負債の発生が見込まれる場合に流動に分類される。本問の社債は流動資産ではなく非流動資産に分類される減債基金から償還されるため、満期が1年以内であっても非流動負債として表示するのが適切である。流動分類は、流動資産を使用しないため定義に反する。社債と減債基金の相殺表示は、流動負債側で示す場合も投資からの控除として示す場合も、相殺権がない限り資産と負債の相殺を禁じる原則に違反する。

questions.subtopic: TDR - Asset Settlement with Two Gain Components
questions.stem: Harlowe Corp. is experiencing financial difficulty. Its lender agrees to accept land in full settlement of a note with a carrying amount of $900,000 (principal of $850,000 plus accrued interest of $50,000). The land has a cost basis of $400,000 and a fair value of $600,000 at the settlement date. What total amount should Harlowe recognize in income as a result of the settlement?
questions.explanation: A troubled debt restructuring settled by transferring an asset produces two separate income components. First, the asset is adjusted to fair value, producing a gain on disposal of $600,000 − $400,000 = $200,000. Second, a restructuring gain is recognized for the excess of the debt's carrying amount (including accrued interest) over the asset's fair value: $900,000 − $600,000 = $300,000. Total effect on income = $200,000 + $300,000 = $500,000. The $200,000 amount captures only the disposal gain, and the $300,000 amount captures only the restructuring gain — each is just one component. The $50,000 amount is merely the accrued interest, which is part of the carrying amount settled, not a separate gain.
questions.explanationJa: 資産の引渡しによる不良債権再編（TDR）の決済では、2つの損益要素が生じる。第一に、資産を公正価値に修正することで処分益 $600,000 − $400,000 = $200,000 を認識する。第二に、債務の帳簿価額（経過利息を含む）が資産の公正価値を超える額をリストラクチャリング利得として認識する: $900,000 − $600,000 = $300,000。損益への合計影響 = $200,000 + $300,000 = $500,000。$200,000 は処分益のみ、$300,000 はリストラクチャリング利得のみで、いずれも一方の構成要素にすぎない。$50,000 は経過利息にすぎず、決済される帳簿価額の一部であって独立した利得ではない。

questions.subtopic: TDR - Future Cash Flows Exceed Carrying Amount
questions.stem: Quillen Corp., a debtor in financial difficulty, restructures a note payable with a carrying amount of $1,000,000. Under the modified terms, the principal is reduced to $950,000 and the interest rate is lowered, so that total future cash payments (principal plus all future interest) equal $1,100,000. How should Quillen account for the restructuring?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognize a $50,000 gain for the principal reduction

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize a $100,000 gain

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize no gain; compute a new effective interest rate and recognize interest expense prospectively

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recognize a $100,000 loss

questions.explanation: In a troubled debt restructuring involving a modification of terms, the debtor compares the total undiscounted future cash payments under the new terms with the carrying amount of the debt. Because the $1,100,000 of total future payments exceeds the $1,000,000 carrying amount, no gain is recognized; the carrying amount remains unchanged, and a new effective interest rate is imputed that equates the future payments to the carrying amount, with interest expense recognized prospectively. A $50,000 gain looks only at the principal reduction and ignores the future interest payments included in the comparison. A $100,000 amount, whether treated as a gain or a loss, is just the excess of future payments over carrying amount — that excess is recognized as interest expense over the remaining term, not as an immediate gain or loss.
questions.explanationJa: 条件変更による不良債権再編では、債務者は新条件下での割引前将来キャッシュ支払総額と債務の帳簿価額を比較する。将来支払総額 $1,100,000 が帳簿価額 $1,000,000 を超えているため利得は認識しない。帳簿価額は変更せず、将来支払額を帳簿価額に等しくする新たな実効利率を算定し、利息費用を将来に向かって認識する。$50,000 の利得は元本減額のみに着目し、比較に含めるべき将来利息支払を無視している。$100,000 は利得・損失いずれの扱いでも将来支払額の帳簿価額超過分にすぎず、この超過分は残存期間にわたる利息費用として認識されるものであって、即時の損益ではない。

questions.subtopic: Fair Value Option - Instrument-Specific Credit Risk
questions.stem: Verlaine Corp. elected the fair value option for a note payable. During Year 1, the fair value of the note decreased by $80,000 (a gain to Verlaine), of which $30,000 is attributable to a deterioration in Verlaine's own (instrument-specific) credit risk. What amount of the fair value change should Verlaine report in net income for Year 1?
questions.explanation: For liabilities measured under the fair value option, the portion of the total fair value change attributable to instrument-specific credit risk is reported in other comprehensive income, and only the remainder is reported in net income. Net income portion = $80,000 − $30,000 = $50,000; the $30,000 credit-risk portion goes to OCI. Reporting the full $80,000 in earnings ignores the required OCI bifurcation and would let a company's own credit deterioration inflate its profits. The $30,000 amount is the piece that belongs in OCI, not net income. Reporting nothing in net income incorrectly routes the entire change through OCI.
questions.explanationJa: 公正価値オプションを適用した負債では、公正価値変動額のうち商品固有の信用リスク（自社の信用リスク）に起因する部分をその他の包括利益（OCI）に表示し、残額のみを純利益に計上する。純利益計上額 = $80,000 − $30,000 = $50,000。信用リスク部分の $30,000 はOCIに計上する。$80,000 全額の純利益計上は要求されるOCIへの区分を無視しており、自社の信用悪化が利益を押し上げる結果を招く。$30,000 はOCIに属する部分であり純利益ではない。純利益への計上をゼロとする処理は変動額全体を誤ってOCIに振り向けている。

questions.stem: A company issued bonds several years ago at a premium and has amortized the premium using the effective interest method. Immediately before repayment at the scheduled maturity date, the carrying amount of the bonds equals:

choices.label: A
choices.text: The face value of the bonds

choices.label: B
choices.text: The face value plus the unamortized premium

choices.label: C
choices.text: The original issue price

choices.label: D
choices.text: The current market value of the bonds

questions.explanation: Premium and discount are amortized to zero over the bond's life, so by the maturity date the carrying amount has converged to the face value — the amount the issuer must repay. This convergence occurs regardless of whether the effective interest or straight-line method is used. A balance of face value plus unamortized premium cannot exist at maturity because the premium is fully amortized by then. The original issue price is the carrying amount only at issuance, before any amortization. Market value is irrelevant to the amortized cost carrying amount of bonds not measured under the fair value option.
questions.explanationJa: 打歩（プレミアム）や割引は社債の存続期間にわたりゼロまで償却されるため、満期日には帳簿価額は額面、すなわち発行者が返済すべき金額に収束する。この収束は実効金利法・定額法のいずれを用いても生じる。満期時点ではプレミアムは全額償却済みのため「額面＋未償却プレミアム」という残高はあり得ない。当初発行価額は償却前の発行時点の帳簿価額にすぎない。公正価値オプションを適用していない社債の償却原価による帳簿価額に、市場価値は関係しない。

questions.stem: On January 2, Vole Co. issued bonds with a face value of $480,000 at a discount to yield 10%. The bonds pay interest semiannually. On June 30, Vole paid bond interest of $14,400. After Vole recorded amortization of the bond discount of $3,600, the bonds had a carrying amount of $363,600. What amount did Vole receive upon issuing the bonds?
questions.explanation: After six months, the carrying amount is $363,600, which equals the original proceeds plus the first period's discount amortization of $3,600. Working backward: $363,600 − $3,600 = $360,000 is the original issue price (proceeds). The bonds were issued at a discount, so proceeds must be below the $480,000 face value, and $360,000 is consistent with that. The cash interest paid of $14,400 is based on the contractual (coupon) rate: $480,000 × coupon rate × 6/12 = $14,400 implies a coupon rate of 6%. The 10% is the yield (market) rate, which is applied to the carrying amount to compute interest expense, not cash interest paid.
questions.explanationJa: 6か月後の帳簿価額$363,600は、発行時の受取額に割引の償却額$3,600を加えた金額です。逆算すると、発行時の受取額は$363,600 − $3,600 = $360,000となります。額面$480,000より低い金額で発行されており、割引発行と一致します。

questions.stem: A corporation recently issued $4 million of 10-year, 3% bonds at 101. There were 200,000 detachable stock warrants included as part of the sale. Each warrant allows the bondholder to purchase one share of no par common stock for $12 per share. On the date of issuance, the stock warrants had a fair value of $1 per warrant. By what amount did the corporation's long-term debt increase as a result of this issuance?
questions.explanation: Total proceeds = $4,000,000 × 101% = $4,040,000. Warrants' fair value = 200,000 × $1 = $200,000. Because only the warrant fair value is known (the bond-only fair value is not separately stated), the incremental method is used under ASC 470-20: allocate the known fair value ($200,000) to the warrants, and assign the remainder to the bonds. Bond proceeds = $4,040,000 − $200,000 = $3,840,000, which is the amount recorded as long-term debt. The warrant portion of $200,000 is credited to additional paid-in capital (equity). (ASC 470-20-25)
questions.explanationJa: 発行総額 = $4,000,000 × 101% = $4,040,000。新株予約権の公正価値 = 200,000 × $1 = $200,000。分離型新株予約権付社債では、発行総額から新株予約権の公正価値を差し引いた金額を社債として計上します。$4,040,000 − $200,000 = $3,840,000が長期負債の増加額です。

questions.stem: If bonds are issued at a premium, total interest expense recorded over the life of a bond equals the cash interest payments

choices.label: A
choices.text: Less the premium.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Less par value.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Plus the premium.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Plus par value.

questions.explanation: When bonds are issued at a premium, the issuer receives more than face value but repays only face value at maturity. As the premium is amortized over the bond's life, it reduces interest expense below the cash coupon payments each period. Therefore, total interest expense over the life of the bond equals total cash interest payments minus the total premium amortized. (ASC 835-30)
questions.explanationJa: プレミアム（割増）発行の場合、発行者は額面を超える金額を受け取りますが、返済は額面のみです。プレミアムは毎期の利息費用を減額する形で償却されるため、存続期間中の利息費用合計 = 現金利払い合計 − プレミアム合計となります。

questions.stem: On March 1, Year 1, Somar Co. issued 20-year bonds at a discount. By September 1, Year 5, the bonds were quoted at 106 when Somar exercised its right to retire the bonds at 105. How should Somar report the bond retirement on its Year 5 income statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A gain in continuing operations.

choices.label: B
choices.text: A loss in continuing operations.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A gain in discontinued operations.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A loss in discontinued operations.

questions.explanation: Because the bonds were issued at a discount, the carrying amount (book value) at September 1, Year 5 is well below face value (100). Only 4.5 years of a 20-year life have elapsed, so very little discount has been amortized; the carrying amount is far below 100, and therefore far below the call price of 105. When the retirement price exceeds the carrying amount, the company pays more to retire the debt than its book value, resulting in a loss. Loss = Call Price − Carrying Amount > 0. The market quote of 106 is a distractor; the settlement price is the contractual call price (105), not the market price. Under ASC 470-50, gains and losses on early extinguishment of debt are recognized as part of income from continuing operations, not as extraordinary items or discontinued operations. (ASC 470-50-40)
questions.explanationJa: 割引発行のため、Year 5時点の帳簿価額（簿価）は額面100を大きく下回っています。20年満期のうち4.5年しか経過していないため、割引額の償却がほとんど進んでおらず、帳簿価額は100をはるかに下回ります。コール価格105 > 帳簿価額 のため、差額は損失（ロス）です。市場価格106はひっかけであり、決済価格は契約上のコール価格105です。ASC 470-50では、債務早期消滅の損益は継続事業の一部として認識し、廃止事業や特別項目としては表示しません。

questions.stem: Grant Co. issued $500,000 face value, five-year, 8% bonds on December 31, Year 1. The bonds pay interest annually and were sold to yield 7%. Present value factors are as follows: 7% 8% Present value of $1, five periods 0.712986 0.680583 Present value of ordinary annuity of $1, five periods 4.100197 3.992710 Present value of annuity due of $1, five periods 4.387211 4.312127 What amount of long-term liability should Grant report on December 31, Year 1, for this sale?
questions.explanation: The bonds pay annual interest of $500,000 × 8% = $40,000. Since the bonds pay interest annually and were sold at the 7% market rate, use the ordinary annuity factor and the lump-sum PV factor at 7% for 5 periods. PV of coupons = $40,000 × 4.100197 = $164,008. PV of principal = $500,000 × 0.712986 = $356,493. Issue price = $164,008 + $356,493 = $520,501. The long-term liability on December 31, Year 1 (date of issuance) equals the issue price of $520,501. (ASC 835-30)

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 2, Oak Co. issued 400 of its 8%, $1,000 bonds at 97 plus accrued interest. The bonds are dated October 1, Year 1, and mature on October 1, Year 10. Interest is payable semiannually on April 1 and October 1. Accrued interest for the period October 1, Year 1, to January 1, Year 2, amounted to $8,000. On January 1, Year 2, what amount did Oak receive from the bond issuance?
questions.explanation: Face value of bonds = 400 × $1,000 = $400,000. Issue price at 97 = $400,000 × 97% = $388,000. Accrued interest collected from buyers = $8,000 (3 months from Oct 1 to Jan 1: 8% × $400,000 × 3/12 = $8,000). Total proceeds received = $388,000 + $8,000 = $396,000. The accrued interest is collected from investors and returned to them on the next interest payment date (April 1); it is recorded separately as a liability (accrued interest payable), not as part of the bond proceeds. (ASC 835-30)
questions.explanationJa: 社債額面 = 400 × $1,000 = $400,000。発行価格97% = $388,000。Oct 1からJan 1の経過利息 = 8% × $400,000 × 3/12 = $8,000を買主から受領。受取総額 = $388,000 + $8,000 = $396,000。経過利息は次回利払日（4月1日）に買主へ返還するため、社債の調達資金とは別に負債として計上します。

questions.stem: Ray Corp. issued 200 of its 8%, ten-year, $1,000 face value bonds for $240,000. Each bond contained 100 detachable stock warrants, each of which was for one share of Ray's common stock at $12 per share. Immediately after issuance, the market value of each warrant was $2, and the market value of the bonds without the warrants was $196,000. What amount of discount on the bonds should Ray record at issuance?
questions.explanation: Total proceeds = $240,000. Total fair value of warrants = 200 bonds × 100 warrants × $2 = $40,000. Fair value of bonds alone = $196,000. Total fair value of both components = $196,000 + $40,000 = $236,000. Using the proportional (relative fair value) method required by ASC 470-20 when both fair values are known: bond allocation = $240,000 × ($196,000 / $236,000) = $199,322. Bond discount = face value − allocated proceeds = $200,000 − $199,322 = $678. The warrants are allocated $240,000 × ($40,000 / $236,000) = $40,678, recorded in equity. (ASC 470-20-25)

questions.stem: Summer Corp. issued $1,000,000 face value bonds with detachable common stock warrants at 103. Immediately after the bonds were issued, the fair value of the warrants was $20,000, but the fair value of the bonds could not be determined. The issue price exceeded the sum of the warrants' fair value and the face value of the bonds. What amount of discount or premium on the bonds should Summer record at issuance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $20,000 discount

choices.label: B
choices.text: $10,000 discount

choices.label: C
choices.text: $10,000 premium

choices.label: D
choices.text: $30, 000 premium

questions.explanation: Total proceeds = $1,000,000 × 103% = $1,030,000. When the fair value of the bonds is not determinable, the incremental method is used: allocate the known fair value ($20,000) to the warrants and assign the remainder to the bonds. Bond proceeds = $1,030,000 − $20,000 = $1,010,000. Since bond proceeds of $1,010,000 exceed the $1,000,000 face value, the bonds are recorded at a premium of $10,000. (ASC 470-20-25)
questions.explanationJa: 発行総額 = $1,000,000 × 103% = $1,030,000。社債の公正価値が不明なため、増分法を使用：新株予約権に公正価値$20,000を配分し、残額$1,010,000を社債に配分。$1,010,000 > 額面$1,000,000のため、$10,000のプレミアム（割増）が計上されます。

questions.stem: When debt is issued at a discount, interest expense over the term of debt equals the cash interest paid

choices.label: A
choices.text: Minus discount.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Minus discount minus par value.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Plus discount.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Plus discount plus par value.

questions.explanation: When debt is issued at a discount, the issuer receives less than face value but must repay the full face value at maturity. The difference (discount) represents additional borrowing cost beyond the coupon payments. As the discount is amortized each period, it increases interest expense above the cash coupon paid. Therefore, total interest expense = total cash interest payments plus the total discount amortized over the life of the debt. (ASC 835-30)
questions.explanationJa: 割引発行の場合、発行者は額面より少ない金額を受け取りますが、満期に額面を返済します。この差額（割引）は追加の資金調達コストです。毎期の割引償却が利息費用を加算するため、利息費用合計 = 現金利払い合計 + 割引合計となります。

questions.stem: On July 31, Year 3, Dome Co. issued $1,000,000 of 10%, 15-year bonds at par and used a portion of the proceeds to call its 600 outstanding 11%, $1,000 face value bonds, due on July 31, Year 13, at 102 and retire them. On that date, unamortized bond premium related to the 11% bonds was $65,000. What amount should Dome report in its Year 3 income statement as gain or loss, before income taxes, from retirement of bonds?
questions.explanation: Carrying amount of old bonds = face value + unamortized premium = (600 × $1,000) + $65,000 = $665,000. Call price = $600,000 × 102% = $612,000. Gain on retirement = $665,000 − $612,000 = $53,000. Because the carrying amount exceeds the retirement price, Dome recognizes a $53,000 gain in income from continuing operations. (ASC 470-50-40)

questions.stem: On June 30, Year 1, City, Inc. issued 2,500 of its 9%, 10-year, $1,000 face value bonds with detachable stock warrants at par. Each bond carried a detachable warrant for one share of City's common stock at a specified option price of $37 per share. Immediately after issuance, the market value of the bonds without the warrants was $2,850,000 and the market value of the warrants was $150,000. What amount of discount on the bonds should City record at issuance?
questions.explanation: Total proceeds (issued at par) = 2,500 × $1,000 = $2,500,000. Face value = $2,500,000. Total fair value of bonds + warrants = $2,850,000 + $150,000 = $3,000,000. Using the proportional method: bond allocation = $2,500,000 × ($2,850,000 / $3,000,000) = $2,375,000. Discount = $2,500,000 face − $2,375,000 allocated = $125,000. (ASC 470-20-25)

questions.stem: On June 1 of the current year, Cross Corp. issued $300,000 of 8% bonds payable at par with interest payment dates of April 1 and October 1. In its income statement for the current year ended December 31, what amount of interest expense should Cross report?
questions.explanation: The bonds were issued June 1 and the year ends December 31, covering 7 months. Interest accrues from June 1 through December 31. Interest expense = $300,000 × 8% × 7/12 = $14,000. The interest payment dates (April 1 and October 1) determine when cash is paid, but the accrual basis requires recognizing interest for the full period from issuance to year-end, which is 7 months. (ASC 835-30)
questions.explanationJa: 社債は6月1日に発行され、決算日は12月31日のため、7か月分の利息が発生します。利息費用 = $300,000 × 8% × 7/12 = $14,000。利払日（4月1日・10月1日）はキャッシュ支払いタイミングであり、発生主義では発行日から決算日まで7か月分を計上します。

questions.stem: Perk, Inc. issued $500,000, 10% bonds to yield 8%. Bond issuance costs were $10,000. How should Perk calculate the net proceeds to be received from the issuance?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Discount the bonds at the stated rate of interest.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Discount the bonds at the market rate of interest.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Discount the bonds at the stated rate of interest and deduct bond issuance costs.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Discount the bonds at the market rate of interest and deduct bond issuance costs.

questions.explanation: The issue price (gross proceeds) is determined by discounting the bond's future cash flows—coupon payments and principal—at the market (effective) interest rate of 8%, not the stated coupon rate of 10%. Net proceeds equal gross proceeds minus bond issuance costs of $10,000. Under ASC 835-30 and ASC 470-20, bond issuance costs are deducted from the face value to arrive at the carrying amount of the liability. (ASC 835-30, ASC 470-20)
questions.explanationJa: 発行価格は将来キャッシュフローを実勢金利（8%）で割り引いて算出します（表面利率10%では算出しません）。純手取り額 = 総手取り額 − 社債発行費$10,000。ASC 835-30・470-20では、社債発行費は負債の帳簿価額から差し引きます。

questions.stem: A bond issued on June 1, Year 1, has interest payment dates of April 1 and October 1. Bond interest expense for the year ended December 31, Year 1, is for a period of

choices.label: A
choices.text: Three months.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Four months.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Six months.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Seven months.

questions.explanation: The bond was issued on June 1, Year 1. Interest expense must be accrued from the issuance date through December 31, Year 1. Counting from June 1 through December 31 gives 7 months. On October 1, Year 1, the first coupon is paid (covering June 1 to October 1 = 4 months), and then an accrual is needed for October 1 through December 31 (3 months). Total = 4 + 3 = 7 months of interest expense for the year. (ASC 835-30)
questions.explanationJa: 社債は6月1日に発行され、決算日は12月31日です。6月1日から12月31日までは7か月です。10月1日に最初の利払い（6月1日〜10月1日の4か月分）があり、10月1日から12月31日まで3か月分を未払計上します。合計7か月分の利息費用が認識されます。

questions.stem: On June 30, Year 1, Adonis Co. had outstanding 4%, $4,000,000 face value bonds, originally issued at 98, maturing on June 30, Year 6. Interest was payable semiannually every June 30 and December 31. Adonis did not elect the fair value option for reporting its financial liabilities. On June 30, Year 1, after amortization was recorded for the period, the unamortized bond discount and bond issue costs were $40,000 and $30,000 respectively. On that date, Adonis acquired all its outstanding bonds on the open market at 97 and retired them. At June 30, Year 1, what amount should Adonis recognize as gain before income taxes on redemption of bonds?
questions.explanation: Carrying amount of bonds = face value − unamortized discount − unamortized issue costs = $4,000,000 − $40,000 − $30,000 = $3,930,000. Retirement (repurchase) price = $4,000,000 × 97% = $3,880,000. Gain on retirement = carrying amount − repurchase price = $3,930,000 − $3,880,000 = $50,000. Because the carrying amount exceeds the retirement price, Adonis recognizes a $50,000 gain from continuing operations. (ASC 470-50-40, ASC 835-30)

questions.stem: An issuer of bonds uses a sinking fund for the retirement of the bonds. Cash is transferred to the sinking fund and subsequently used to purchase investments. The interest and dividends earned in the sinking fund are: (1) Added to fund balance and (2) Reported as income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: (1) Yes, (2) Yes

choices.label: B
choices.text: (1) Yes, (2) No

choices.label: C
choices.text: (1) No, (2) Yes

questions.explanation: When a sinking fund earns interest and dividends, those earnings are added to the sinking fund balance (increasing the fund asset) and simultaneously reported as income (investment income) on the issuer's income statement. The sinking fund is a restricted asset, and its earnings increase both the fund balance and the entity's income. (ASC 835-30; general investment income accounting)
questions.explanationJa: 社債償還積立金（シンキングファンド）が得た利息・配当は、積立金残高に加算されると同時に、発行者の損益計算書において収益（投資収益）として計上されます。積立金は制限付き資産であり、その収益は積立金残高と当期利益の両方を増加させます。

questions.stem: On January 31, Year 2, Beau Corp. issued $300,000, 12% bonds at par value. The bonds are dated December 31, Year 1, and mature on December 31, Year 10. Interest will be paid semiannually on June 30 and December 31. What amount of accrued interest payable should Beau report on its September 30, Year 2, balance sheet?
questions.explanation: The last interest payment date before September 30 is June 30. Accrued interest at September 30 covers July 1 through September 30 = 3 months. Accrued interest = $300,000 × 12% × 3/12 = $9,000. Note: the bond was issued January 31 but dated December 31, Year 1; on January 31 issuance, Beau collected 1 month of accrued interest from buyers. Interest payment dates drive the accrual calculation at September 30. (ASC 835-30)
questions.explanationJa: 9月30日時点では、最後の利払日（6月30日）から9月30日まで3か月が経過しています。未払利息 = $300,000 × 12% × 3/12 = $9,000。1月31日発行・12月31日付の社債の発行時経過利息は買主から回収済みであり、9月30日の未払利息は3か月分です。

questions.stem: Young Co. issues $800,000 of 10% bonds dated January 1, Year 1. Interest is payable semiannually on June 30 and December 31. The bonds mature in five years. The current market for similar bonds is 8%. The entire issue is sold on the date of issue. The following values are given: Present value of ordinary annuity Present value of $1 N = 10; I = 0.04 8.11090 0.67556 N = 10; I = 0.05 7.72173 0.61391 What amount of proceeds on the sale of bonds should Young report?
questions.explanation: Since interest is semiannual and the market rate is 8% annually, use a semiannual rate of 4% (I = 0.04) over 10 periods (N = 10). Semiannual coupon = $800,000 × 10% / 2 = $40,000. PV of coupons = $40,000 × 8.11090 = $324,436. PV of principal = $800,000 × 0.67556 = $540,448. Total proceeds = $324,436 + $540,448 = $864,884. (ASC 835-30)

questions.stem: Gains or losses from the early extinguishment of debt should be

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognized in income before taxes in the period of extinguishment.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognized in income before taxes over the life of the debt, using the retrospective method.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Amortized over the life of the new debt issue.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Amortized over the remaining original life of the extinguished issue.

questions.explanation: Under ASC 470-50, gains or losses from early extinguishment of debt are recognized immediately in income from continuing operations in the period in which the extinguishment occurs. They are not deferred, amortized over any future period, or treated as extraordinary items. This reflects the economic reality that the transaction is complete in the current period. (ASC 470-50-40)
questions.explanationJa: ASC 470-50に基づき、債務の早期消滅による損益は、消滅が発生した期の継続事業の損益として即時認識します。将来の期間に繰り延べたり、償却したりすることはありません。

questions.stem: On January 1, a company issued a $50,000 face value, 8% five-year bond for $46,139 that will yield 10%. Interest is payable on June 30 and December 31. What is the bond carrying amount on December 31 of the current year?
questions.explanation: Semiannual coupon = $50,000 × 8% / 2 = $2,000. Semiannual market rate = 10% / 2 = 5%. Period 1 (Jan 1 – Jun 30): Interest expense = $46,139 × 5% = $2,307; discount amortization = $2,307 − $2,000 = $307; carrying amount after period 1 = $46,139 + $307 = $46,446. Period 2 (Jul 1 – Dec 31): Interest expense = $46,446 × 5% = $2,322; discount amortization = $2,322 − $2,000 = $322; carrying amount after period 2 = $46,446 + $322 = $46,768. (ASC 835-30 effective interest method)

questions.stem:
Foley Co. is preparing the electronic spreadsheet below to amortize the discount on its 10-year, 6%, $100,000 bonds payable. Bonds were issued on December 31 to yield 8%. Interest is paid annually. Foley uses the effective interest method to amortize bond discounts.

|   | A    | B         | C                | D                     | E               |
|---|------|-----------|------------------|-----------------------|-----------------|
| 1 | Year | Cash paid | Interest expense | Discount amortization | Carrying amount |
| 2 | 1    |           |                  |                       | $86,580         |
| 3 | 2    | $6,000    |                  |                       |                 |

Which formula should Foley use in cell E3 to calculate the bonds' carrying amount at the end of Year 2?

choices.label: A
choices.text: E2 + D3

choices.label: B
choices.text: E2 − D3

choices.label: C
choices.text: E2 + C3

choices.label: D
choices.text: E2 − C3

questions.explanation: Under the effective interest method, each period's carrying amount increases by the amount of discount amortized (column D). Starting from the beginning-of-year carrying amount in E2, the end-of-year carrying amount is E2 plus the discount amortization in D3, so the correct formula is E2 + D3. The discount amortization each period equals interest expense (column C) minus cash paid (column B), and the carrying amount increases as the discount is consumed. (ASC 835-30)
questions.explanationJa: 実効利子法では、各期の帳簿価額は割引償却額（D列）だけ増加します。期首帳簿価額E2に当期割引償却額D3を加えた値が期末帳簿価額となるため、正しい数式はE2 + D3です。

questions.stem: When the effective interest method of amortization is used for bonds issued at a premium, the amount of interest payable for an interest period is calculated by multiplying the

choices.label: A
choices.text: Face value of the bonds at the beginning of the period by the contractual interest rate.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Face value of the bonds at the beginning of the period by the effective interest rates.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Carrying value of the bonds at the beginning of the period by the contractual interest rate.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Carrying value of the bonds at the beginning of the period by the effective interest rates.

questions.explanation: The question asks about interest payable (cash to be paid), not interest expense. The cash coupon payment is determined by the face value and the contractual (stated) interest rate, regardless of whether the bonds were issued at a premium or discount. Interest payable = Face value × stated rate. Interest expense (for premium/discount amortization purposes) is separately calculated using the carrying value × effective rate. (ASC 835-30)
questions.explanationJa: この設問は「支払利息（未払利息）の計算」について問うています。現金利払額は額面と表面利率から計算します。支払利息 = 額面 × 表面利率。一方、利息費用（実効利子法）は帳簿価額 × 実効利率で計算します。設問は「interest payable」なので額面 × 表面利率が正解です。

questions.stem:
During Year 1, Lake Co. issued 3,000 of its 9%, $1,000 face value bonds at 101 1/2. In connection with the sale of these bonds, Lake paid the following expenses:

| Expense | Amount |
|---------|--------|
| Promotion costs | $20,000 |
| Engraving and printing | 25,000 |
| Underwriters' commissions | 200,000 |

What amount should Lake record as bond issue costs to be amortized over the term of the bonds?
questions.explanation: All three costs—promotion costs ($20,000), engraving and printing ($25,000), and underwriters' commissions ($200,000)—are considered bond issuance costs under ASC 835-30 and are recorded as a direct deduction from the bond liability's carrying value, then amortized over the term of the bonds using the effective interest method. Total = $20,000 + $25,000 + $200,000 = $245,000. (ASC 835-30-45)
questions.explanationJa: プロモーション費用$20,000、版彫刷費用$25,000、引受手数料$200,000のいずれも社債発行費（ASC 835-30）に該当します。合計 = $20,000 + $25,000 + $200,000 = $245,000。社債発行費は社債帳簿価額の直接控除として計上し、社債期間にわたって実効利子法で償却します。

questions.stem: In open market transactions, Gold Corp. simultaneously sold its investment in Iron Corp. bonds classified as trading and purchased its own outstanding bonds. The broker remitted the net cash from the two transactions. Gold's gain on the purchase of its own bonds exceeded its loss on the sale of the Iron bonds. Gold should report the

choices.label: A
choices.text: Effects of the two transactions in other comprehensive income (OCI).

choices.label: B
choices.text: Effects of the two transactions in income from continuing operations.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Effect of its own bond transaction gain in income from continuing operations and report the loss from the Iron bond transaction in OCI.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Effect of its own bond transaction in OCI and report the loss from the Iron bond transaction loss in income from continuing operations.

questions.explanation: Both transactions flow through income from continuing operations. The gain on early extinguishment of Gold's own bonds is recognized in continuing operations per ASC 470-50. The loss on selling Iron Corp. bonds classified as trading securities is also recognized in earnings (income from continuing operations) per ASC 320-10, since trading security gains and losses bypass OCI and go directly to net income. (ASC 470-50, ASC 320-10)
questions.explanationJa: 自社社債の早期消滅利得はASC 470-50に基づき継続事業の損益に計上されます。また、売買目的有価証券（トレーディング）の処分損失はASC 320-10に基づき純利益に直接計上されます（OCIを経由しません）。したがって、両取引ともに継続事業の損益として報告します。

questions.stem: On May 1, Year 1, Bolt Corp. issued 11% bonds in the face amount of $1,000,000 that mature on May 1, Year 10. The bonds were issued to yield 10%, resulting in a bond premium of $62,000. Bolt uses the effective interest method of amortizing bond premiums. Interest is payable semiannually on November 1 and May 1. What amount should Bolt report as the unamortized bond premium in its October 31, Year 1, balance sheet?
questions.explanation: Carrying amount at issuance (May 1, Year 1) = $1,000,000 + $62,000 = $1,062,000. For the first semiannual period (May 1 to November 1): semiannual effective rate = 10% / 2 = 5%. Cash coupon = $1,000,000 × 11% / 2 = $55,000. Interest expense = $1,062,000 × 5% = $53,100. Premium amortized = $55,000 − $53,100 = $1,900. The October 31 balance sheet date falls at the end of the full first interest period (the November 1 payment date is the period end, and October 31 is effectively after all amortization has been recorded for that period). Unamortized premium at October 31 = $62,000 − $1,900 = $60,100. (ASC 835-30 effective interest method)

questions.stem:
Potter Co. has the following contingencies, all resulting from lawsuits in progress during the current year:

| Item | Amount |
|------|--------|
| Probable loss contingency | $1,500,000 |
| Reasonably possible loss contingency | 500,000 |
| Remote loss contingency | 300,000 |
| Probable gain contingency | 700,000 |

How much should be disclosed in Potter's notes to the financial statements for the current year?

*(Choices show: Contingent loss / Contingent gain)*
questions.explanation: Under ASC 450-20, loss contingencies that are probable (must be accrued and disclosed) and reasonably possible (disclosed but not accrued) must both be disclosed in the notes. Remote contingencies require no disclosure. Therefore, disclosure covers probable losses ($1,500,000) plus reasonably possible losses ($500,000) = $2,000,000. Gain contingencies (ASC 450-30) are never accrued, but probable gains may be disclosed; however, disclosing a probable gain of $700,000 is appropriate. Total disclosed: contingent loss $2,000,000, contingent gain $700,000. (ASC 450-20, ASC 450-30)
